title: Python Basics — Day 01
description: Variables, print, input, and data types.

exercises.test: Write code that prints Hello World
exercises.concept: Use print() with quotes around the text to display it.

exercises.test: Ask for user name and print greeting
exercises.solution:
name = input("What is your name? ")
print("Hello, " + name + "!")
exercises.concept: Use input() to read text, then print() to greet.

exercises.test: Print the sum of two numbers
exercises.concept: Assign numbers to variables, then print their sum.

exercises.test: Print the data type of a variable
exercises.concept: Use type() to check what kind of data a variable holds.

exercises.test: Read age from user and print its type
exercises.concept: input() always returns a string, even for numbers.
